false_positive_details: COHERENCE-NEW-001 (conciseness, repeated reviewer recommendation): Discarded. Repetition of recommendation from Key Findings to Methodology is structurally appropriate for a research summary document format. Evidence did not support treating this as a meaningful quality defect.

validators_summary.structural_enumerator: No new findings. All structural issues from iteration 1 resolved.
validators_summary.structural_adversary: No new findings. Range '8-12% absolute accuracy' examined and confirmed defensible — low end from raw data (8.37pp), high end from source characterization (~12%). No error.
validators_summary.accuracy_enumerator: All factual errors from iteration 1 resolved. Meta-Judge finding now correctly states 2 reviewers, ~8-12% improvement, 3rd reviewer decreases performance. PoLL correctly characterized. All figures verified against framework.json ground truth.
validators_summary.accuracy_adversary: No new findings. Reviewer framing terminology ('systematic enumeration vs. adversarial skepticism') not independently cited but represents authors' synthesis consistent with cited research. Not flagged.
validators_summary.coherence_enumerator: Threshold consistent (95) throughout. Reviewer count consistent (2) throughout. PoLL characterization aligned with Conclusions. One candidate finding (repetition) discarded as false positive.
validators_summary.coherence_adversary: Document reads as coherent unified whole. No jarring inconsistencies remain. Validation threshold attribution ('based on our analysis of the cited research') is marginally vague but not a new finding — improvement over iteration 1 state.

note: Score 100/100 reflects zero remaining evidence-backed findings. All 7 findings from iteration 1 were resolved in the fix pass.
